corrections.text: Four sources are not on the project's source_allowlist.txt: docs.mesa3d.org (used three times for the official Mesa release notes — a primary authoritative source), gamingonlinux.com (reputable Linux gaming news outlet covering open-source graphics), linuxcompatible.org (Linux-focused news aggregator; content corroborated by primary sources), and khronos.org (the Khronos Group — the standards body behind Vulkan and OpenGL, a highly authoritative primary source). All were fetched successfully (HTTP 200) and their content verifies the article's claims. Recommend adding docs.mesa3d.org, gamingonlinux.com, khronos.org, and linuxcompatible.org to config/source_allowlist.txt.

The Overview section describes VirGL as 'the legacy OpenGL-over-Vulkan translation layer'. VirGL is more precisely a virtualized OpenGL driver that renders through virglrenderer onto a host Vulkan stack; it is not a general OpenGL-over-Vulkan translation layer in the same sense as Zink. The description is a minor imprecision but is not materially misleading in context, as it is followed by an accurate explanation of VirGL's VM use case. No correction to the article text required; noted for editorial awareness.
